name: La Cumbre (Fernandina)
eruptionHistory: The most active volcano in the Galápagos. Erupts every few years with effusive lava flows. The 1968 eruption caused dramatic caldera collapse.
description: La Cumbre on Fernandina Island is the most volcanically active of the Galápagos volcanoes and sits on one of the most pristine major islands, home to flightless cormorants and marine iguanas.
